title: I Shall Wait, Looking Forward to Your Arrival!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, lyric cadence, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The Tamil below is a source-checked reading copy; the frozen source chapter remains unchanged and authoritative. Source pages confirm the OCR-omitted opening `உடன்பிறப்பே,`, `செப்டம்பர் 15!`, and the clean sign-off without the OCR ellipsis. Clearly mechanical word breaks are repaired without modernising the source. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement.
paragraphs: Only three days remain before this year’s Mupperum Vizha, our Festival of Three Great Celebrations. Whether it is a conference or a protest, I usually write four or five letters inviting every Kazhagam Udanpirappu. This Mupperum Vizha is conducted each year on behalf of headquarters. It brings together the 136th birthday of Thanthai Periyar, the radiant sun of rationalism; the 106th birthday of Perarignar Anna; and the 66th birthday of the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam. We combine these three occasions and celebrate them every year as the Mupperum Vizha.
September 15!

It is the 106th birthday of Perarignar Anna, who impressed upon our memory and engraved in our hearts the guiding mantra of Duty, Dignity and Discipline; the peerless sovereign of the Tamil people, the one answer to the question of who that sovereign is; a man of wisdom, a mountain of self-respect, a great forum for Tamils across the world, the very grammar of excellence, and a treasure of virtue who showed compassion even to his enemies! On that very day, we are conducting this year’s Mupperum Vizha in Chennai.

At about 7.30 a.m. on 15 September, General Secretary Professor, Treasurer Thalapathi Stalin, other leading members of the Kazhagam and I will, as usual, garland Anna’s statue opposite Valluvar Kottam in Chennai and pay our respects.

That evening, Kalaignar Arangam at Anna Arivalayam will host every part of the programme: the presentation of the Mupperum Vizha awards; financial assistance to students from the DMK Trust; prizes from the Murasoli Trust; and cash awards and medals for outstanding autorickshaw drivers.

Anna showed immense affection and concern for students throughout his life and worked for their advancement. As a mark of that commitment, the DMK Trust will present financial assistance and certificates on the Mupperum Vizha stage to students who secured high marks in the Higher Secondary examination.

As in previous years, the Murasoli Trust will provide financial assistance and certificates to college and Higher Secondary students selected at the State level in the competition to recite the poems of Revolutionary Poet Bharathidasan. Nor is that all. To demonstrate Anna’s affection for workers, certificates of merit, cash awards and medals will again be presented to outstanding autorickshaw drivers.

These events will be followed by the presentation of the Mupperum Vizha awards. Our General Secretary, the Professor of Tamil dignity, will preside. I shall present the Thanthai Periyar Award to veteran Kazhagam Udanpirappu Bengaluru V.T. Shanmugam, one of those who built the Kazhagam in Karnataka; the Anna Award to Kazhagam Udanpirappu Mrs Munavar Jan; the Pavendar Bharathidasan Award to Mrs Pudukkottai Vijaya, who has carried Kazhagam propaganda to towns and villages from an early age; and the Kalaignar Award, bearing my name, to our Udanpirappu from the world of art, Kumarimuthu, who serves the movement without expectation of reward.

South Chennai District Kazhagam Secretary, younger brother and MLA J. Anbazhagan, will deliver the welcome address. Treasurer and younger brother Thalapathi M.K. Stalin, Principal Secretary Arcot N. Veerasamy, Deputy General Secretaries Duraimurugan, V.P. Duraisamy and Mrs Sarguna Pandian will offer felicitations.

The presentation of awards to distinguished Kazhagam workers at the Mupperum Vizha has continued without interruption for even a single year since 1985. At first, only three awards were presented: the Periyar Award, Anna Award and Kalaignar Award. From the year 2000, the Pavendar Award was added, and four awards have been presented annually. This year marks the thirtieth award ceremony. So far, these awards have been given to 101 people. I have already named the recipients in this thirtieth year.

Periyar Award recipient Bengaluru V.T. Shanmugam has worked in the Kazhagam treating its growth as his very breath of life. Believing that the Kazhagam must have its own headquarters building in Karnataka, he laboured tirelessly to construct a three-storeyed building in Bengaluru’s Ramachandrapuram and name it “Kalaignaragam.” At a time when no branch of the Kazhagam owned a building, he also worked immensely to construct a three-storeyed mansion in the Srirampuram-Sudhandira Nagar area of Bengaluru and name it “Kalaignar Arivalayam.” In recognition of the Kazhagam service rendered by so distinguished a person, headquarters is presenting him with this year’s Periyar Award.

Anna Award recipient Mrs S.A. Munavar Jan developed an interest in public service while still young. Realising that the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam was the fitting place for such work, she joined it and has served ever since. She participates without fail in every people’s welfare protest, procession, meeting and conference announced by headquarters and the district Kazhagam. She has been arrested twice for taking part in protests. In every election, she throws herself wholeheartedly into going from house to house, gathering votes for our candidates and working for their victory. In appreciation of her distinguished Kazhagam service, headquarters will present her with the Anna Award.

Pavendar Award recipient Mrs Pudukkottai Vijaya has made proclaiming Kazhagam principles her duty. She has campaigned in towns and villages across Tamil Nadu; there is scarcely a town in the State where she has not spoken. She served as Propaganda Committee Secretary of the Women’s Wing, Deputy Secretary of the Pudukkottai District Kazhagam, and once again as Women’s Wing Propaganda Committee Secretary. She now continues to serve as Secretary of the Women’s Wing. In recognition of her work, headquarters will present her with the Pavendar Award at the Mupperum Vizha.

This year, the Kalaignar Award given in my name is being presented to comedian Kumarimuthu. While still young, he heard Perarignar Anna speak and became an admirer of the Kazhagam. Because he was the younger brother of the celebrated film actor Nambirajan, Nadigavel M.R. Radha took him into his theatre company. His distinctive laugh greatly increased his fame. By displaying his acting ability in films, he earned the titles Kalaimamani, Kalaiselvam and Kalaivanar. The Dravidar Kazhagam, that training ground of rationalism, honoured him with its Periyar Award. To add further distinction to Kumarimuthu’s achievements and appreciate his service, this year’s Kalaignar Award is being presented to him.
Each award recipient will receive a shield and a cash purse of Rs. 50,000 from headquarters.
Udanpirappē,

I know that reports about these celebrations have already appeared in the newspapers and that you already intend to attend. Even so, would you not feel that your elder brother had failed to invite you to Anna’s celebration? That is why I write this letter!

I know very well that even while headquarters conducts its celebration for Anna, you will honour Anna in your own town to the fullest extent possible. That will shine like a lamp placed upon a hill. Even so, prepare to travel with your family to the celebration conducted by headquarters in Chennai!
If my younger brothers believe that not even a grain of sand’s worth of distress should reach their leader’s mind,
with a face wearing Anna’s smiling serenity,
with a stride, chest held high, like Ayya,
even in these years of age, the vigour of youth
will surely return to me, my younger brother!
So I take heart. Even if fire spreads from all eight directions, even if the wicked cast their nets every day, even if thousands upon thousands of arrows come speeding toward me, I shall not worry:
duty alone shall be my vision,
the Kazhagam my battle camp,
Periyar my companion,
and Perarignar Anna the other half of my life.

I remind you again of what I once wrote as this Mupperum Vizha approached. For thirty years, I have continuously taken part in the festival and had the opportunity, with my own hands, to present these awards to the Kazhagam’s courageous workers, to those who have laboured for the Kazhagam. When I think of that, how could my happiness have any limit?

Only three days remain. Chennai District Kazhagam Secretary, younger brother Anbazhagan, and the headquarters team are making the arrangements together. As in every year, I shall wait, looking forward to your arrival, so that you may make this year’s Mupperum Vizha splendid too!
With affection,
M.K.
12 September 2014
